sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Genoa have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 1 win, 1 draw, and 3 losses, managing to score in most games but struggling defensively with no clean sheets in their last 3. Fiorentina, on the other hand, are in poor form, with 3 losses and 1 draw in their last 5, failing to win and conceding consistently without a clean sheet in 4 games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, Fiorentina have had the upper hand, winning both matches (2-1 and 1-0). Genoa have struggled to keep a clean sheet against Fiorentina, conceding in 8 straight meetings, with both teams often scoring in recent clashes.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Genoa, sitting 18th with 6 points, have underperformed offensively (6 goals, 19th in league) but are strong in ground duels (3rd) and tackles (1st). Fiorentina, at 20th with 4 points, have also struggled to score (7 goals) and are winless this season, with defensive frailties evident as they concede frequently.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Genoa's home advantage at Luigi Ferraris could be crucial, as they’ve shown resilience in creating big chances (4th in league). Fiorentina's missing key forward Moise Kean might hinder their attack, while their tendency for high-card games (5/7 over 4.5 cards) suggests a physical battle.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore's prediction (37% home win, 35% draw, 28% away win) and Genoa's slightly better recent form, they hold a marginal edge. Fiorentina’s historical dominance in this fixture and late-game scoring ability (50% of goals after 60 minutes) could make it close, but Genoa’s defensive solidity might secure a narrow result.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The match between Genoa and Fiorentina at Luigi Ferraris has been evenly poised with a 1-1 scoreline at half-time. Both teams have shown moments of quality, with Genoa taking the lead through a headed goal by Leo Østigård early on, followed by Fiorentina's equalizer via a penalty converted by Albert Guðmundsson. The game has been competitive, with both sides creating a big chance each and managing an equal number of shots on target.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team statistics comparison
halftimeSections.text: Fiorentina has held a slight edge in possession and has shown more dominance in duels, winning a higher percentage of both aerial and ground challenges. Their expected goals figure also suggests they’ve had more threatening opportunities. Genoa, however, has been more effective in penetrating the penalty area with a higher number of entries and has shown intent in the final third with a decent number of successful passes. Fiorentina's discipline has been an issue with two yellow cards compared to none for Genoa.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players at the break
halftimeSections.text: For Genoa, Leo Østigård has been pivotal, not only scoring the opener but also contributing defensively with several clearances and an interception. Aarón Martín has also stood out with an assist and key passes that have created dangerous moments. On the Fiorentina side, Albert Guðmundsson’s composure from the penalty spot has kept them in the game, while Niccolò Fortini has impressed with his involvement in build-up play and defensive contributions. Both teams rely on these players to drive their momentum in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attacking momentum between the two sides. Genoa started brightly with early attacking spells, pushing forward with intent. Fiorentina responded with a sustained period of dominance, taking control for a significant stretch and eventually earning their penalty. Genoa regained momentum later in the half with several strong attacking phases, especially towards the end of the first 45 minutes. However, Fiorentina managed a brief resurgence just before the whistle, showing they remain a threat.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Genoa has focused on exploiting the flanks and delivering crosses into the box, as evidenced by their goal and frequent penalty area entries. Their defensive structure has held firm without conceding fouls leading to cards. Fiorentina, on the other hand, has relied on structured build-up play through the middle and final third entries, though they’ve struggled to convert possession into clear chances. Their higher number of corners indicates a potential set-piece threat that Genoa must be wary of.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Fiorentina push harder for a lead, given their higher expected goals and possession advantage in the first half. Genoa, however, will look to capitalize on counter-attacks and maintain their effectiveness in the penalty area. Both teams have shown enough attacking intent to suggest more goals are possible. Fiorentina’s disciplinary issues could play a role if Genoa exploits spaces left by overcommitted challenges.
